name: Garlic-Stuffed Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ Tablespoons Fresh Parmesan Cheese, Grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
2. Clean the mushrooms by removing stems and reserving them.
3. Parboil the mushrooms in just enough water to cover for 3 minutes, until barely cooked. Drain well, blot with paper towels, and set aside until needed.
4. If mushroom stems appear woody, trim away the tip. Chop stems coarsely.
5. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a skillet. Toss chopped mushrooms stems and scallions. Saute until tender and most of the mushroom juices have evaporated. Add pecans, bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, and remaining tablespoon of butter. Stir until the butter is melted and absorbed. Stir in garlic bechamel.
6. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
7. Stuff each mushroom cap with the mixture, mounding it neatly over the top. Sprinkle each with some cayenne pepper.
8. Place stuffed mushrooms in a lightly oiled, shallow baking dish. Bake for 10 minutes or until heated through. Serve immediately.
Garlic Bechamel:
1. Melt butter in a heavy saucepan and whisk in flour. Cook roux over low heat, stirring constantly for 3 to 4 minutes.
2. Whisk in scalded milk. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and cook gently, stirring frequently, for 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Add salt and pepper and whisk in garlic puree. Remove from the heat.
4. Beat egg in a bowl. Beat some sauce into the egg. Next, beat the egg mixture back into the garlic sauce. Taste and correct seasonings.
5. Store in the refrigerator with plastic wrap placed directly on the surface of the sauce until needed. Thin with milk before using if necessary.
